Title: Home Ministry: Refugee status no shield against Malaysian laws
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Home Ministry today stressed that refugee status does not provide immunity from Malaysian laws.
In a statement, the ministry said any individual who commits a crime, threatens public safety or violates the country's laws remains subject to enforcement action, prosecution and the legal process.
"Refugee status does not provide immunity from Malaysian laws. Any individual who commits a criminal offence, threatens public safety or violates the laws of the country remains subject to enforcement action, prosecution and the applicable legal process," it said.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid: Muslims must be innovators, not just tech consumers
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Muslims must become thinkers and creators of innovation, and not remain merely consumers of technology, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
Zahid said the practice of Islam, particularly in the Asean region, which is characterised by moderation, courtesy and social diversity, could offer a model of ethical technology that does not erode the values and identity of the Muslim community.
"Ethical technology does not emerge solely from systems, but from those who possess knowledge, values and wisdom.
"That is why national development cannot focus only on physical and economic growth. The more important foundation is human capital development.
"We want to produce a generation that is knowledgeable, principled, skilled and capable of making sound judgments," he said in his speech at the 4th Southeast Asian Scholars Conference at the Kuala Lumpur Convention Centre (KLCC) today.
Zahid, who is also Rural and Regional Development Minister, said Muslims today face challenges such as false information crossing national borders and extremist ideologies spreading through digital spaces.
"Economic imbalances place pressure on societies, while humanitarian crises, including the suffering of the Palestinian people, continue to test the conscience of Muslims and the global community.
"In this situation, Muslim scholars in the region must build consensus on key issues, namely safeguarding peace, preserving social harmony, developing ethical technology, elevating knowledge as the foundation of policy, and upholding Islam as rahmatan lil alamin (a mercy to all creation).
"However, consensus must be translated into action and not remain merely words," he said.
He hopes that this year's Southeast Asian Scholars Conference would pave the way for the establishment of a permanent network of scholars in the region, involving the publication of joint policy papers, cross-border research, academic exchanges and regular dialogue platforms between scholars and policymakers.
"It is an Intellectual Diplomacy agenda that must be advanced. We should not merely discuss theories, but also have the courage to help solve the problems faced by the ummah.
"It is diplomacy that resolves misunderstandings, eases tensions, connects knowledge with policy, and leads the future with mature arguments, high standards of conduct and clear objectives," he said.
He said that if the Intellectual Diplomacy agenda could be realised, Southeast Asia would be able to offer a model of Islam that is knowledgeable, civilised and relevant to contemporary challenges.
"A model that is not extreme, not detached from reality, and one that embraces progress while preserving human values," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pejuang to contest limited seats in Johor and Negri Sembilan polls
Article:
KOTA BARU: Parti Pejuang Tanah Air (Pejuang) will field candidates in the Johor and Negri Sembilan elections but will contest only a limited number of seats to ensure more organised and realistic preparations.
Its president Datuk Seri Mukhriz Mahathir said the party was interested in contesting in both states but would take a cautious approach based on its capabilities.
"We are very interested in fielding candidates in Johor and Negri Sembilan. At the same time, we are measuring ourselves according to our own capabilities, so we will not contest many seats," he said.
He said Pejuang was open to cooperation with other parties and aimed to avoid clashes with political allies.
"We hope not to clash with our political allies, which is why discussions have already begun, although they have yet to be finalised," he said.
Mukhriz said this after officiating the Ketereh and Pasir Puteh parliamentary cluster branch opening ceremony and presenting appointment letters to branch chiefs in Kampung Setek, Ketereh, today.
He said preparations for the state elections were easier as the party did not intend to contest many seats or aim to form a state government independently.
"When we are not contesting many seats, preparations are easier," he said.
Mukhriz said the party had held discussions with three political parties, while talks with others were ongoing.
"There are also overlapping constituencies, so we need to compromise," he said.
He declined to disclose the number of seats targeted, saying Pejuang would only contest a small number of selected constituencies.
"We just want to plant the seeds and offer voters choices alongside other larger parties," he said.
Mukhriz said the party's principle in negotiations was to avoid splitting the Malay vote, as suggested by Tun Dr Mahathir Mohamad.
"We want to ensure the Malay vote in particular is not divided," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid rebukes Nga for criticising EC over Johor, Negri poll dates
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has hit out at his cabinet colleague Nga Kor Ming over the DAP leader's criticism of the Election Commission's decision to hold the Johor and Negri Sembilan elections on separate dates.
Zahid, who is also deputy prime minister, said political leaders should respect the EC's authority in managing elections.
Asked about the housing and local government minister's remarks, Zahid said the EC had the power and discretion to determine election dates.
"No one, especially those who have served as members of the cabinet, should express such personal views.
"More so if the person is a senior leader of a party that is part of the government," he said after attending the 4th Southeast Asian Scholars Conference 2026.
Yesterday, the EC announced that the Johor election would be held on July 11, while Negri Sembilan would go to the polls on Aug 1.
Nga, the DAP deputy chairman, reportedly said the two state elections should be held concurrently and that the EC chairman should be held accountable.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Civil Defence Force takes over five hours to transfer obese patient to hospital
Article:
KOTA BARU: The Civil Defence Force took more than five hours to transfer an obese woman experiencing breathing difficulties in a special operation last night.
The operation by the Rantau Panjang Civil Defence Force (APM) involved moving a 180kg patient from Kampung Banggol Che Dol, Pasir Mas.
Rantau Panjang APM officer Lt (PA) Mohd Fuad Ismail said the patient was first taken to Pasir Mas Hospital for treatment before being referred to Raja Perempuan Zainab II Hospital (HRPZII) in Kota Bharu.
He said the operation began at 10.45pm and ended at 4.30am today.
"The transfer operation used a lorry equipped with a hydraulic system to facilitate safe lifting and movement of the patient," he said when contacted.
Fuad said the operation involved six APM personnel and four hospital staff, with medical assistance provided throughout.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'We still support Hamzah': MIPP skips Reset convention for Johor polls
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian Indian People's Party (MIPP) president P. Punithan said that his party is currently campaigning for the Johor state election.
He said that the party's absence from the 'Reset' Convention in Kelantan today is not a sign they do not support the movement founded by Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainuddin.
Punithan said he has openly shown his support for the movement.
He said that the organisers of Reset, including Hamzah himself, had been informed of his absence well in advance.
"I'm in Johor for the election campaign programmes. This has been communicated to the Reset team and Hamzah himself.
"We congratulate them and wish them the best of luck. We hope to continue moving forward together with Perikatan Nasional (PN)," he told Berita Harian today.
Punithan said that MIPP views Reset as an important platform to discuss various issues concerning national reform, governance, economic resilience, and the future direction of Malaysia.
He added that the inaugural convention would serve as an arena to spark new ideas, strengthen unity, and contribute to efforts in building a more prosperous and inclusive nation.
Hamzah today announced his new political party,
Parti Wawasan Negara
, saying its name was proposed by Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang.
Speaking at the Reset Convention in Tanah Merah, Hamzah said the party's annual general meeting is expected to be held next week.
Hamzah, a former opposition leader,
launched the Reset movement
on Feb 14, a day after he was sacked from Bersatu following a leadership dispute with President Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin.
Among those in attendance at the convention today are Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud; Gerakan president Datuk Dr Dominic Lau; Kelantan Pas commissioner Datuk Ahmad Yakub; former Melaka Bersatu chairman Tan Sri Abdul Rahim Thamby Chik; and former Bersatu information chief Datuk Wan Saiful Wan Jan.
Also present are Masjid Tanah member of parliament (MP) Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin; Beluran MP Datuk Seri Dr Ronald Kiandee; and Machang MP Wan Ahmad Fayhsal Wan Ahmad Kamal.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Umno Youth leader calls Loke's Johor train ride invitation 'political theatre'
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno Youth deputy chief Mohd Hairi Mad Shah said Transport Minister Anthony Loke's invitation to Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi was political theatre.
Hairi said Loke's invitation for Onn Hafiz to join him on a KTM Komuter train ride during a Transport Ministry programme in Johor gave the impression that the state government had been unwilling to cooperate.
"Previously, he had gone down to inspect and visit the RTS Link project with the DAP machinery and not once did he invite the Johor menteri besar.
"But yesterday, there was a pretence of openness in inviting the menteri besar to sit and ride the train together. This is political drama and theatre being played out," he said in a statement.
Loke is DAP secretary-general.
Hairi claimed many official ministry programmes and visits in Johor had not previously included formal invitations to Onn Hafiz or state government representatives.
"Therefore, when an open invitation is now made to the Johor state government, I question the sincerity and consistency in the effort to truly cooperate and sit at the same table," he said.
He said the state government had not politicised the issue and had instead focused on development and the welfare of the people.
"Johor's political landscape has its own realities. Following the 2022 state election, Barisan Nasional secured a clear mandate from voters, winning 40 of the 56 state seats, with the state government being formed based on that mandate.
"Throughout this administration, there has been no form of power-sharing in the state government with DAP. There have been no appointments of village heads, local councillors or state administrative machinery from the party. These are facts that must be understood by all parties."
Hairi said Johor had always practised a culture of consensus and professionalism, and that cooperation must be based on sincerity and mutual respect rather than political rhetoric.
"Politics is dynamic, but mature politics requires professionalism and respect for institutions and governments elected by the people. If there is a genuine intention to cooperate, it should be done through proper channels such as official invitations and mutual respect, not merely open statements that sound more like political rhetoric than sincerity."
Last night,
Loke invited Onn Hafiz
to join him on a KTM Komuter train ride during his ministry's programme in Johor on Tuesday.
The invitation, made in his capacity as transport minister, followed recent remarks by Onn Hafiz, who reportedly said he would rather not hold a position than sit at the same table with DAP.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Agency warns of water tumblers with hidden compartments for vapes, contraband
Article:
KUALA NERUS: The National Anti-Drugs Agency (Nada) has detected water tumblers with hidden compartments used to conceal electronic cigarettes or vapes, which are being openly sold on online platforms.
Nada Prevention Division director Siti Mariam Mursidan urged parents to monitor their children's belongings to prevent vaping, which she said can be a gateway to drug abuse among teenagers and students.
She said vaping among the younger generation must be curbed early, as it could lead to more serious addiction.
"Nada, as a preventive agency, believes vaping must be stopped from the start because drug addiction often begins with smoking and vaping.
"Parents must know what their children are bringing. Communication is key, not just focusing on gadgets," she said after launching the Terengganu-level Drug-Free Aspirations Tour at Bandar Al Wathiqu Billah Shah today.
She was commenting on a viral video showing water tumblers that appear normal but contain secret compartments to hide prohibited items, including vapes and mobile phones.
She said the use of such tumblers among students was uncovered by a secondary school teacher in the Klang Valley.
Siti Mariam said parental monitoring should not mean restricting teenagers, but guiding them away from unhealthy habits, including substance abuse.
She said Nada officers are actively engaging school students nationwide to discourage behaviours linked to drug use, including smoking and vaping.
On national drug abuse statistics this year, she said 191,832 drug users were recorded, with a national rate of 560 per 100,000 population.
She said four border states recorded the highest rates: Kelantan (1,105 per 100,000), followed by Terengganu (997), Perlis (970) and Kedah (965).
Other states with high rates include Pahang (795), Penang (757), Melaka (659), Perak (579) and Negri Sembilan (553).
Sabah recorded the lowest rate at 230 per 100,000, followed by Putrajaya (276), Selangor (338), Labuan (451), Kuala Lumpur (488), Sarawak (498) and Johor (499).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UDPATED] RM5mil allocated to revitalise statesmen memorials
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The government will consider allocating a RM5 million matching grant under the 2027 Budget to revitalise 10 statesmen memorials nationwide, including in Sabah and Sarawak, the prime minister announced.
Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said that prominent figures such as Tunku Abdul Rahman Putra Al-Haj, Sabah's Tun Mustapha Harun and Tun Fuad Stephens, who represent different ethnicities, shared similar traits in ensuring the country's success.
""Our nation's founding fathers had a vision of nationhood, even though it was not expressed in sophisticated language or through complex theories.
"When I look at their backgrounds, they were not scholars or intellectuals in the way we understand those terms today. Rather, they were leaders with a deep love for the nation.
"They represented small ethnic and community groups, yet they possessed a broad vision of how to preserve the spirit of esprit de corps — a sense of togetherness, solidarity and belonging," he said during the National Unity Week at Likas Sports Complex here.
Present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Fadillah Yusof, Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor as well as Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang.
Anwar said that for Malaysians who want this country to remain peaceful, they understand the value, blessings and benefits of unity.
"It brings stability and serves as a strong foundation for driving economic growth.
"However, because we have become accustomed to living in peace and harmony, we sometimes forget that there are occasions when we can fall under influences that erode this spirit and foster sentiments that run contrary to unity.
"We must not become complacent or assume that unity will naturally prevail under all circumstances without continuous efforts to nurture it and to prevent the emergence of forces that can divide us."
"That is why we established a ministry dedicated to this cause, and I would like to congratulate the National Unity Ministry for doing its best over the past few years to strengthen and promote this."
He also announced that the government will provide an activity grant of RM10,000 to 189 Rukun Negara Secretariats at higher learning institutions nationwide to organise nation-building, patriotism and unity programmes for university students.
Grants of RM5,000 to 6,849 Rukun Negara Clubs in schools across the country to promote the practice and appreciation of the Rukun Negara principles from an early stage of education.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Plot twist: Hadi says Hamzah to be reappointed opposition leader
Article:
TANAH MERAH: Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin will be reappointed opposition leader, Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang announced today.
This was said by Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang at the Reset convention held at the Kota Harmoni Expo site here.
Hadi said the decision was made through consultation among Pas leaders and members of parliament (MPs), who unanimously agreed that Hamzah should continue leading the opposition bloc in Parliament.
"Not only Pas MPs, but MPs aligned with the Reset movement have also agreed.
"Pas has decided that Hamzah will continue as Opposition Leader," he said at the closing of the convention.
Hadi also announced that Perikatan Nasional (PN) would retain Terengganu Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar as the coalition's chairman.
He said the decisions were made by consensus in the interest of stability and continuity within the opposition coalition.
On May 16, it was reported that
Samsuri had been appointed the new opposition leader
following Hamzah's sacking from Bersatu.
Hadi also said several prominent political figures, including former ministers, senior Umno leaders and representatives from non-governmental organisations, were expected to join Pas.
"Tonight in Negri Sembilan, we will announce several prominent figures from political parties, including former state executive councillors, who will be joining Pas," he said.
Meanwhile, speaking at a press conference later, Hadi said PN was prepared to accept the new party Parti Wawasan Negara as a component of the coalition.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hamzah pitches Reset agenda as path to national renewal
Article:
TANAH MERAH: Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin said the Reset agenda is a people-centred movement aimed at rebuilding Malaysia on the principles of morality, integrity and good governance.
The former Bersatu deputy president said the movement sought to unite Malaysians across political divides to address national challenges and restore prosperity for future generations.
"Reset is a movement for reform. It is a struggle guided by a moral compass to rebuild the nation based on principles and values.
"It calls on all Malaysians, regardless of political affiliation or state, to work together to ensure Malaysia returns to being a prosperous and principled nation," he said at the launch of the Reset Convention here today.
Hamzah, who heads the Reset Malaysia movement, said the initiative was driven by concerns over the country's political and economic direction.
He said Malaysia had undergone several periods that required a reset and criticised the current administration for failing to address the people's hardships effectively.
"Today, our country is being led by leaders who do not seem to understand the difficulties faced by ordinary Malaysians.
"The Reset agenda is the people's agenda. It is about political renewal and economic reform to improve incomes and quality of life," he said.
Hamzah said the movement's goal was to make Malaysia peaceful, stable and respected internationally while strengthening the economy.
"We want Malaysia to emerge from its current problems and move away from being trapped as a low-income nation," he added.
Earlier, Hamzah expressed gratitude for the support he had received following his departure from Bersatu, describing Pas leaders as among his closest allies.
He paid tribute to Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang, whom he described as a respected Islamic scholar in Malaysia and abroad.
"Many people do not realise that we have a great Islamic scholar whose influence extends beyond our country. I have often sought his advice and guidance.
"If we want success, we need the guidance of ulama. He is not only a scholar for Malaysia but for the world," he said.
Hamzah also welcomed support from political leaders and parties for the Reset initiative, saying it reflected a growing desire for greater political maturity and national consensus.
Also present were Perikatan Nasional chairman and Terengganu Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar, Gerakan president Datuk Dr Dominic Lau Hoe Chai and Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump's name removed from Kennedy Center in predawn operation
Article:
WASHINGTON: Workers stripped US President Donald Trump's name from the Kennedy Centre early on Saturday, less than six months after it went up, complying with a judge's ruling that the performing arts landmark cannot be renamed without an act of Congress.
The work began about 1.20am (0520 GMT), hours after the Department of Justice said the government would miss the court-ordered deadline of 11.59pm on Friday to take Trump's name off the Washington venue, created a half-century ago to honour an assassinated president.
The centre's board, which Trump chairs, voted in Dec to rename it The Donald J. Trump and The John F. Kennedy Memorial Centre for the Performing Arts. Workers began affixing his name to the building the next day.
REMOVAL FOLLOWS MISSED DEADLINE, COURT FILINGS
After erecting scaffolding late on Friday, workers draped tarps over the temporary structure in the predawn hours and were seen removing letters about 3.10am in an operation that took about 30 minutes.
Late on Friday, the DOJ had said in a court filing it would miss the deadline because of thunderstorms that could pose safety risks for the workers, seeking a 12-hour extension.
Democratic US Representative Joyce Beatty of Ohio, who brought the lawsuit that forced Trump's name to be removed, called the request to extend the two-week-old deadline "inexcusable" and part of "a pattern of non-compliance," according to the DOJ filing.
The centre opened in 1971 as a memorial to President John F. Kennedy, a Democrat assassinated in 1963. Trump, a Republican, has packed its board of trustees with allies since resuming office last year.
Hours before the DOJ filing, a federal judge in Washington had declined the department's request to pause an order to remove Trump's name.
US District Judge Christopher Cooper said he would not lift the order while a federal appeals court considers his ruling that only Congress could rename the venue. The administration appealed that order to the US Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia, which also rejected the government's request for a pause on Friday.
The White House and the Kennedy Centre did not immediately respond to requests for comment.
Cooper ruled on May 29 that only Congress could rename the arts centre. His order had required Trump's name to be removed from the building's facade, its website and other materials.
In urging the appeals court to pause the order, the DOJ said: "It does not make sense to alter the Centre's name and signage now, only to potentially revert the name again after what should be a successful appeal."
Trump in February announced a two-year closure of the centre for a major renovation. He has made a broader push to reshape Washington's monumental core, including plans for a 250ft (75m) arch and a 90,000 sq ft (8,400 sq m) ballroom on the site of the East Wing of the White House, which Trump had demolished in Oct.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Mothers key to nation's progress
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The role of mothers and women within the family institution is fundamental to a nation's progress and the development of a society that is moral, knowledgeable and resilient, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
He said that regardless of a country's physical and technological advancements, human development that begins within the family remains the key factor in nurturing a good society.
"This is where the role of mothers and women lies. No matter how advanced a country becomes, early education that originates from the family institution, particularly a mother's upbringing, forms the foundation of a good society.
"If that foundation is built well by parents, it will be easier for us to create a healthy and vibrant Madani nation," he said.
He was speaking at the Melangkah Bersama Madani: Wanita Beriman, Wanita Bertahan programme for Sabah at the Sabah International Convention Centre (SICC) here today.
Also present were Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Sabah and Sarawak Affairs) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud, and Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Marhamah Rosli.
Anwar said national development should not focus solely on infrastructure, technology, industry and artificial intelligence (AI), but must also be balanced with the development of values, morals and inner strength.
He said the Malaysia Madani concept introduced by the government places emphasis on human development alongside material progress.
"We do not merely want to build skyscrapers, factories and infrastructure. We also want to develop people with values, morals and awareness.
"Skyscrapers, modern technology, industry, information technology and AI must begin with self-development, values, character and awareness of the hereafter," he said.
He said the most important foundation of education begins at home through the role of parents in instilling religious values and responsibility in their children.
"The school system may succeed, but the foundation of education begins at home. At home, children come to know their parents, learn the Quran and Hadith, understand their religious responsibilities and learn to pray," he said.
On the role of women in Islamic history, Anwar said figures such as Sayidatina Khadijah RA and Sayidatina Asma' Abu Bakar RA demonstrated that women have always played a major role in the struggle and development of the Muslim community.
He said Sayidatina Khadijah was a source of strength and support for Prophet Muhammad SAW when he received the first revelation, while Sayidatina Asma' played an important role in assisting the Prophet's migration to Madinah.
"In every important chapter of Islamic history, women were never left behind. Their contributions have been immense in the family, struggle, economy, politics and nation-building," he said.
At the same time, he reminded the public not to neglect the family institution and their children's education in the pursuit of progress.
Anwar also called on Malaysians to embrace patience in facing life's challenges, adding that every family would face its own tests.
"Raising children requires patience. Building a family requires patience. Developing a society requires patience. Even nation-building requires patience coupled with wisdom.
"With patience, knowledge and piety, we can produce a generation of noble character and build a Madani nation that succeeds in this world and the hereafter," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Illegal structures at Rohingya settlement being demolished [WATCH]
Article:
HULU LANGAT: Illegal structures at a Rohingya settlement in Kampung Baru Sungai Makau are being demolished, with the process expected to be completed in two weeks.
Hulu Langat member of parliament Mohd Sany Hamzan said he was informed by the village chief that demolition work began today.
He said the two-week timeframe was requested by the landowner following discussions with the village chief and was not imposed by any government agency.
"I was informed that the demolition involves the single-storey structures at the rear of the site and not the three-storey building that has been widely described as a flat in viral social media postings.
"The timeframe was requested by the landowner himself," he said when contacted.
Sany said the action followed the issuance of a Notice 7A by the Hulu Langat Land and District Office.
He said a Notice 7B could be issued under existing legal provisions if the instructions were not complied with.
"Based on the information received, the area at the rear that is currently being demolished involves land that falls partly under private ownership and partly under government reserve land, while the three-storey building stands on privately owned land," he said.
He added that relevant agencies had been informed to monitor the demolition works and ensure safety requirements and waste disposal procedures were complied with.
Sany said a site inspection found that part of the demolition had already been carried out by the landowner in an effort to comply with the authorities' directive.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kelantan MB: Pas made right call to sever ties with Bersatu
Article:
TANAH MERAH: Pas made the right decision to end its political cooperation with Bersatu, Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud said.
Nassuruddin said Pas could no longer work with Bersatu due to the party's attitude of preventing other parties from joining Perikatan Nasional (PN).
"People are saying Bersatu is fractured. Out of their 25 members of parliament, six have been sacked and nine suspended. Only six MPs remain active.
"If a house is supported by 25 pillars, and 19 of those pillars are removed, what do you think will happen to the house, even if the upper structure looks intact? It will inevitably collapse.
"I do not understand Bersatu's actions. They target their own people, and they also targeted our allies in Pas when the Perlis menteri besar was removed," he said.
He was speaking at the Reset Convention today, which was officiated by Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang.
Also present were Reset movement founder Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainuddin, PN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar, and Gerakan president Datuk Dr Dominic Lau.
Nassuruddin added that Bersatu's rigid stance defeated the spirit of expanding the opposition coalition.
"We want to build a large, strong family and achieve unity among the ummah. But when others wanted to join our home, Bersatu blocked them, and that is why we parted ways.
"The action taken by Pas is correct. We want political cooperation within PN to remain open to all parties, regardless of the size of their support base.
"Only through an open and inclusive approach can we successfully capture Putrajaya in the next general election," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two women arrested over dead baby outside PJ shelter
Article:
PETALING JAYA: Police have arrested two women in their 20s after a dead baby was found in front of a baby shelter on Jalan Bukit Gasing in Petaling Jaya, yesterday.
A post-mortem examination revealed that the baby was miscarried and had been dead for more than 24 hours.
Petaling Jaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsudin Mamat said police received a report on the discovery at 8.10am.
"Neither woman has a prior criminal record," Shamsudin said in a statement today, adding that police also seized several items at the scene.
A magistrate granted a two-day remand order for the first suspect, which expires tomorrow (June 14), and a three-day remand order for the second suspect, which expires on Monday (June 15).
The case is being investigated under Section 318 of the Penal Code for concealment of birth by secret disposal of a dead body.
The offence carries a maximum penalty of two years' imprisonment, a fine, or both, upon conviction.
Anyone with information can come forward to the nearest police station or contact the Petaling Jaya police headquarters operations room at 03-7966 2222.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Saifuddin: Refugees not the same as illegal migrants
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said refugees must be distinguished from undocumented migrants and that the country must balance law enforcement with humanitarian considerations.
Saifuddin said refugees fall into a separate category because of conflict in their countries of origin.
"Refugees are not the same as illegal migrants. Illegal migrants fall under the Immigration Act, but refugees are in a different category because of conflict-driven factors in their countries," he told reporters after attending the "Jelajah Aspirasi Bebas Dadah" programme at the SMK Puteri Wangsa Hall today.
He said Malaysia was developing its own refugee database to strengthen enforcement and improve the accuracy of national records.
"What this means is we will have our own data. If there are violations of Malaysian law, enforcement action can be taken based on that information," he said.
Saifuddin said refugees remain subject to Malaysian law and can be prosecuted if they commit offences.
However, he said the government must also uphold humanitarian principles while pursuing long-term solutions through international cooperation.
The Rohingya are a predominantly Muslim minority from Myanmar's Rakhine State who have fled decades of persecution and violence.
The United Nations describes them as one of the world's most persecuted minorities.
Malaysia is not a signatory to the 1951 Refugee Convention or its 1967 Protocol, and refugees are generally managed through administrative arrangements involving the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR).
Saifuddin said the UNHCR registers refugees and facilitates their resettlement to third countries.
However, he acknowledged that actual resettlement numbers remain significantly lower than registration figures.
Saifuddin also rejected claims that the refugee issue had emerged only under the current administration, describing it as a long-standing challenge.
"This is a legacy issue. It is unfair to place all responsibility on the government of the day," he said, adding that constructive proposals from all stakeholders would be considered.
Yesterday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the government would take action against Rohingya refugees found violating Malaysian laws and regulations, including offences involving business activities and illegal structures.
He said Myanmar nationals, particularly the
Rohingya community,
must comply with all local regulations, stressing that businesses and buildings must operate within the law.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Hamzah launches Parti Wawasan Negara, says Hadi proposed party's name
Article:
TANAH MERAH: Former Bersatu deputy president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainuddin today announced the formation of a new political party, to be known as Parti Wawasan Negara, saying its name was proposed by Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang.
Speaking at the Reset Convention here, Hamzah said the party's annual general meeting is expected to be held next week.
"The name Parti Wawasan Negara was given by Ayah Chik.
"If it is not guided and nurtured by 'Ayahanda' (referring to Hadi), and if it is not given proper direction, we do not know where it will end up," he said.
Hamzah said the new party would work closely with Pas, Gerakan and other like-minded political parties to strengthen opposition cooperation.
"We will stand together with Pas, Gerakan and other parties.
"InsyaAllah, we will build a more comprehensive political cooperation.
"We will also help establish a stronger political foundation for the future of the rakyat and the nation as a whole," he said.
Hamzah described the formation of Parti Wawasan Negara as part of broader efforts to consolidate political forces committed to governance reform, national development and the welfare of Malaysians.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: Maid assaulted on camera after child abuse accusation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Videos showing a domestic helper being assaulted by family members after she was accused of beating their daughter have gone viral on social media.
The clips, uploaded by X user @update11111, show two women and two men confronting the maid while presenting what they claimed was evidence of her actions before allegedly taking turns slapping and assaulting her.
It is understood that the incident took place in Johor.
According to the post, the employer allegedly witnessed the maid hitting the child through closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage.
"Even if it's true, there is a proper way to handle the matter. File a police report and take action through the proper channels.
"Now the issue is that the employer and his wife are accused of assaulting the domestic helper, allegedly causing her serious injuries. Wrong is wrong, let the authorities investigate and take the necessary action," the post read.
The videos have sparked widespread discussion online, with many social media users condemning the alleged assault.
One user commented: "Even if the maid did hit the child, taking the law into your own hands is not the answer. Let the authorities handle the matter."
Another wrote: "I understand the anger, but what were those recording the incident trying to prove? Were they proud of what was happening? The employers could end up facing legal action as well."
The New Straits Times has reached out to police for comment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Strong global ties, political stability secure Malaysia's energy supply – Anwar
Article:
SIPITANG: Malaysia's strong international relations and political stability have enabled the country to secure continued access to oil and gas supplies despite growing uncertainty from the global energy crisis
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia's prudent diplomatic approach had allowed nations such as Russia and Turkmenistan to regard the country as a close and trusted partner.
Recalling his recent official visit to Turkmenistan, Anwar said the country's leadership had expressed willingness to collaborate with Malaysia through Petronas to operate one of the world's largest gas fields.
"Our country maintains friendly relations with all nations, including the United States and China. At the same time, our domestic political situation remains stable, without upheaval or racial discord," he said when officiating the groundbreaking ceremony of the Sabah Southern Madani Link (SSML) project here today.
Also present were Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali, and Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Sabah and Sarawak Affairs) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud.
Anwar said political stability was key to enabling the government to focus on its development agenda while continuing to attract foreign investment.
"That is why I have continued to support Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor. We have had enough disputes over many issues, but the country's politics must remain stable because without stability, investors will not come," he said.
Anwar also said the world was facing growing uncertainty due to energy supply disruptions arising from conflicts in the Middle East, which had affected shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and driven up global oil prices.
He said the situation had forced several countries, including some Asean members, to take drastic measures, including closing about 400 petrol stations due to supply shortages.
"We are grateful that despite these circumstances, fuel prices remain low... in Malaysia, RON95 remains at RM1.99 per litre.
"We adjusted diesel prices slightly, but not for sectors such as agriculture, buses and public transportation because we must ensure that, whatever the challenge, the burden does not fall on the people," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Speed up scheduling of synthetic drug chemicals under Poisons Act
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Authorities and regulators must fast-track the scheduling of synthetic drug chemicals under the Poisons Act 1952 to ensure unimpeded legal action against offenders.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said swift action was critical, warning that delays in gazetting these substances would severely hamper police investigations.
"When a police investigation traces a case back to synthetic drugs, the matter is referred to the Chemistry Department. If toxic elements are identified but happen to be missing from the poisons registry, it severely complicates any subsequent legal proceedings," he said.
He was speaking at a press conference after attending the Aspirasi Bebas Dadah Tour, organised by the National Anti-Drugs Agency (Nada), at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Puteri Wangsa today.
Saifuddin was responding to comments by Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay, who had called for a total ban on vapes following the emergence of a new synthetic drug known as "Piu Piu", which was found laced in e-cigarette liquids.
Ayob Khan had said the drug contains a mixture of Fentanyl and psychoactive chemicals, which can trigger a severe, zombie-like high in users.
Saifuddin said the issue of classifying toxic substances had been brought to the Cabinet to find a permanent solution.
Following this, the Home Ministry is stepping up coordination with the Health Ministry and the Chemistry Department to ensure more effective law enforcement against new synthetic drug syndicates.
He also revealed that national drug data mapping identified the country's East Coast as the hardest-hit area for synthetic drug use, with the trend starting in Kelantan and Terengganu before moving into Pahang.
While the West Coast still records the use of traditional substances such as marijuana and heroin, he noted that the East Coast is now dominated by synthetic drugs, which have a far more devastating impact.
Saifuddin said the country has recorded alarming statistics in domestic security, with a total of 192,000 individuals confirmed to be involved in drug and substance abuse nationwide.
This is according to an integrated data profile combining records from the Health Ministry, private rehabilitation centres, Nada rehab clinics, and the police narcotics division.
Based on the overall data, the national average stands at 560 drug abusers per 100,000 residents, with consumption trends heavily dominated by dangerous synthetic drugs.
Commenting on the situation in Johor, he said the state recorded an average of 499 people involved in drug abuse per 100,000 residents.
Although the state average sits below the national benchmark, Mersing recorded the highest rate in Johor at 1,200 individuals per 100,000 residents, followed by Pontian, Kota Tinggi, and Segamat.
He added that there are currently 30 drug rehabilitation centres nationwide managed by Nada, housing 5,000 clients.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Reset convention draws 20,000-strong crowd
Article:
TANAH MERAH: The inaugural 'Reset' Convention drew an overwhelming response today, with attendance reaching nearly 20,000 people — double the organisers' initial target.
Organisers from the 'Reset' movement had earlier projected a crowd of about 10,000 for the gathering, which was held at Kota Harmoni Expo Site here.
Tanah Merah member of parliament Datuk Seri Ikmal Hisham Abdul Aziz described the turnout as a strong show of support for the movement's aspirations and reform agenda.
"We are grateful and overwhelmed by the response. We initially expected around 10,000 attendees, but the turnout has reached almost 20,000.
"This gathering is not meant to attack or oppose anyone. It is a peaceful assembly for people to come together harmoniously and discuss the future of the country," he said.
Ikmal also expressed appreciation to Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang for agreeing to officiate the convention.
"We in Tanah Merah are very happy and deeply touched that Tan Sri Abdul Hadi was willing to attend and officiate this event," he said.
Abdul Hadi is expected to officially open the convention later today.
Among the prominent leaders present were Perikatan Nasional chairman and Terengganu Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar, Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud and Gerakan president Datuk Dr Dominic Lau Hoe Chai.
Also in attendance were Kelantan Pas commissioner Datuk Ahmad Yakub, former opposition leader and Reset movement founder Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin, former Bersatu Melaka chairman Tan Sri Abdul Rahim Thamby Chik and former Bersatu information chief Datuk Wan Saiful Wan Jan.
Other attendees included Masjid Tanah MP Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin, Beluran MP Datuk Seri Dr Ronald Kiandee and Machang MP Wan Ahmad Fayhsal Wan Ahmad Kamal.
The convention is expected to feature several key announcements on the Reset movement's future direction and political plans.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar approves immediate RM250,000 infrastructure allocation for Sipitang schools
Article:
SIPITANG: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today approved an immediate special allocation of RM250,000 for infrastructure development, including repairs on basic facilities at secondary schools in the Sipitang district.
He said the implementation of the assistance and the disbursement of funds would be coordinated immediately by the Implementation Coordination Unit of the Prime Minister's Department.
"For school facilities, I am approving RM250,000 immediately. I do not want a project to be approved in June 2026 and only begin in August 2027.
"That is an outdated attitude," he said when officiating at the groundbreaking ceremony of the Sabah Southern Madani Link project here today.
Also present were Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali, and Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Sabah and Sarawak Affairs) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud.
Anwar said representatives from the relevant agencies must visit the affected schools within two weeks to assess their needs and expedite the assistance for the comfort of teachers and students.
He also called on all Sabahans to ensure the relationship and cooperation between the federal and state governments remain strong to sustain development continuity.
Political stability at both the state and federal levels, he explained, was the most important pillar enabling the government to focus fully on improving public welfare.
Anwar stressed that the time had come to end prolonged political disputes as they only harmed the state and deterred foreign investment.
"That is why I think enough is enough with all these disputes over various issues. The country's politics must be stable. If there is no stability, people will not invest and we cannot focus on development," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Don't rely on opposition's weaknesses to win state polls, Fahmi tells PH
Article:
KUALA PILAH: Pakatan Harapan (PH) must rely on its own strengths rather than banking on the opposition's weaknesses to secure victories in the upcoming Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections.
PH communications director Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the opposition's vulnerabilities should not compromise the coalition's election machinery preparations or lead to complacency.
"A weak opponent does not mean we are strong. The leadership has directed all Pakatan Harapan machinery and teams to build solid ground.
"We must possess our own distinct strengths and not lean on the shortcomings of our rivals or any other parties in this struggle," he told reporters after visiting the National Information Dissemination Centre (Nadi) in Seri Menanti today.
Fahmi, who is also the communications minister, was commenting on PH's confidence ahead of the dual state polls.
The PKR central leadership council member said the coalition would hold fast to this philosophy while continuing to assess shifting political dynamics.
"We must evaluate political dynamics from time to time, but Pakatan Harapan remains our core foundation," he said.
Fahmi added that PH would ensure its party machinery and communications teams move swiftly, campaign effectively with facts, and recognise that modern voters consume information across multiple channels.
"Voters rely on both mass media and social media. Our teams must have the capability to engage voters effectively beyond face-to-face interactions.
"Seat allocations for Johor and Negri Sembilan have been finalised. Candidate screening is underway and will be concluded soon, with names expected to be announced within a week," he said.
On the potential spread of fake news and fabricated party logos during the campaign period, Fahmi said his ministry would collaborate with the Malaysian Media Council to curb misinformation, particularly on social media platforms.
"Social media platforms must take immediate action. We have already observed state election-related misinformation on platforms like TikTok.
"We will hold discussions with the Malaysian Media Council and plan to meet with social media platform providers to coordinate our response. I hope to make an announcement regarding this ahead of National Journalists' Day," he said.
The Election Commission has scheduled the Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections to run on separate timelines.
For Johor, nomination day is set for June 27, followed by early voting on July 7 and polling day on July 11.
Nomination day for Negri Sembilan is scheduled for July 18, with early voting on July 28 and polling day on August 1.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hazmat team deployed after chemical spill in Kuantan
Article:
KUANTAN: A hazardous materials (Hazmat) team was deployed after a tanker carrying hydrochloric acid overturned near the Cherating bridge here today, causing part of its load to spill into a nearby mangrove swamp.
The incident also left the lorry driver injured.
Pahang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Mohd Salahuddin Isa said the Gebeng fire station received an alert at 7.32am and dispatched nine firefighters to the scene.
"The lorry, which was transporting the chemical from Pasir Gudang, Johor, to Kemaman, Terengganu, landed on its side, resulting in a minor leak.
"Firefighters were informed that the injured driver had been taken to Kemaman Hospital for treatment," he said in a statement.
Salahuddin said firefighters inspecting the site found a leak from the storage tank's manhole cover, with hydrochloric acid flowing into a nearby mangrove swamp.
"A water curtain was deployed to prevent acid vapours from spreading towards the main road.
"The Department of Environment and the company involved were notified to assist with the removal of the chemical, while the Hazmat team monitored the situation at the site. The volume of the spill is estimated to be less than 1,000 litres," he said.
Salahuddin said a crane would be used to lift the overturned tanker before the remaining chemical was transferred to a replacement tanker.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Asyraf Wajdi says poor students will get help, urges them to accept offers
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki has urged students from poor and underprivileged backgrounds not to reject university offers due to financial concerns, assuring them that assistance is available.
Asyraf said he had received numerous messages from students, including orphans and those from low-income families, who were considering turning down public university offers because of the costs involved.
He said he had since contacted several vice-chancellors and discussed the matter with Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir, who instructed universities to assist students facing financial difficulties.
"I have received many messages on social media regarding orphans and students from poor families who are considering rejecting offers from public universities.
"I have contacted several vice-chancellors, and Minister Dr Zambry has instructed universities to help students facing financial difficulties. Do not reject the offers," he said in a Facebook post today.
Asyraf also assured students who received offers from Mara institutions, including Kolej Mara, Kolej Profesional Mara, TVET Mara, Kolej Poly-Tech Mara, Universiti Poly-Tech Malaysia and Universiti Kuala Lumpur, that support would be provided to those in need.
He said education remained a key pathway to transforming lives and breaking the cycle of poverty, adding that Mara would continue supporting deserving students.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Sabah southern link project reflects federal commitment to Sabah's interior, says PM
Article:
SIPITANG:  The Sabah Southern Madani Link (SSML) electricity grid project reflects the federal government's commitment to ensuring infrastructure development reaches communities in Sabah's interior.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the project was in line with the Malaysia Madani policy, which emphasises balanced and equitable development rather than concentrating progress solely in major urban centres.
He stressed that development could not have a meaningful impact if basic amenities remained inaccessible to communities in remote areas, particularly in Sabah's interior.
"That is why I am determined to ensure that development is distributed across all parts of the state, including rural and interior areas.
"This is what distinguishes our approach from that of some other countries, where development is concentrated only in major cities," he said when officiating the groundbreaking ceremony for the SSML project here.
Also present were Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali, and Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Sabah and Sarawak Affairs) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud.
Anwar said many of those criticising development allocations for Sabah and Sarawak had never visited the ground to witness firsthand the challenges faced by local communities, including poor road conditions and issues related to water and electricity supply.
"It is our responsibility to explain this to them and ensure that priority continues to be given to these allocations to guarantee essential amenities for the people and drive industrial growth here," he said.
Meanwhile, the prime minister said the implementation of the SSML project would also catalyse a major transformation of Sabah's power grid, enabling the state to play a significant role in realising the Asean Power Grid initiative aimed at linking electricity supply systems across Southeast Asia.
He said the integration would involve the development of a regional energy interconnection network stretching from Vietnam via subsea cables to Peninsular Malaysia and Singapore, before being linked with Sarawak's subsea power network.
"With the enhanced capacity of the Sabah Grid, we will be able to connect electricity supply from Sarawak to Sabah and channel it onward to Kalimantan in Indonesia and subsequently to the southern region," he said.
Anwar said the development of an integrated power grid would not only improve the reliability of electricity supply in Sabah, but also bring significant benefits to the people through better infrastructure, a more stable energy supply and stronger appeal to investors.
"Who would want to invest if our areas are left in the dark or if the power supply is insufficient to support industry? That is why this international-scale infrastructure is being developed to ensure the state's economic growth can be accelerated more effectively," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hundreds gather at Thai royal palace to bid farewell to Princess Bajrakitiyabha
Article:
BANGKOK: Hundreds of black-clad Thai mourners gathered at the royal palace in Bangkok on Saturday, awaiting the arrival of the body of the king's eldest daughter a day after her death was announced.
Princess Bajrakitiyabha, known as Princess Bha, died aged 47 on Thursday after spending more than three years in a coma due to a cardiac condition.
"I will wait here all day for her body to arrive," Donnapha Kladbupha, a 54-year-old English teacher and self-described royalist, told AFP.
"When it comes to saying goodbye, it's not easy for us," she said, adding that the monarchy represented "unity" for Thai people during times of distress.
Donnapha joined around 20 fellow members of an online royalist group in what she called an effort "to give support to my king."
Nearby, mourners queued for their turn to perform a Buddhist ritual of pouring holy water into a ceremonial bowl placed before a portrait of the princess.
"I'm sad that she passed away, especially when I saw her pictures as a child," another mourner, Nitikan Tephakham, 79, from the northeastern province of Roi Et, told AFP.
"When she was sick, I prayed for sacred beings to protect her and hoped for a miracle," she added.
- STRICT PROTOCOL -
The princess' body is due to be transferred from Chulalongkorn Hospital, where she was treated, to the Grand Palace on Saturday afternoon – a customary procedure for deceased members of the royal family.
Thai police and public transport operators warned people to expect heavy traffic along the procession route and advised motorists to avoid affected areas where possible.
When King Bhumibol Adulyadej passed away in October 2016, his body was also taken to the Grand Palace, where it lay in state before an elaborate royal cremation ceremony the following year.
The Thai government has yet to announce details of the princess' funeral, but it has instructed state officials to wear black and ordered that flags be flown at half-mast for 15 days.
The proceedings were carried out under tight security and strict protocol, where attendants wore formal attire, media access was restricted and dozens of police officers stood guard.
Tourists at the palace grounds, one of Bangkok's most popular attractions, also had to find workarounds.
"It's something quite unique that someone can be this much loved," Maria Marcais, a 22-year-old visitor from Canada.
"It's profound," she said.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Azalina to seek 2027 Budget allocation to expand rural legal aid satellite offices
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said will seek an allocation under the 2027 Budget to expand Legal Aid Department (JBG) satellite offices to rural areas nationwide.
She said the move is important to ensure that the public, particularly those in remote and rural areas, have easier access to legal aid services and are aware of their rights to legal protection and assistance.
"When we talk about legal aid, we must provide protection to the people so that they know they have the right to obtain legal assistance.
"That is why I will apply for an allocation in next year's budget. If we can establish more satellite offices in districts located far from state offices, it will make it easier for the public to access legal aid services," she told reporters after officiating the JBG Satellite Office at the Pengerang Court in Bandar Penawar here today.
Azalina, who is also the Pengerang member of parliament, said the service would be further strengthened through the use of JBG mobile vans under the Justice on Wheels (JOW) programme to reach villages and remote areas, including in Sabah and Sarawak.
She said the approach is in line with the government's aspiration to bring legal aid services directly to the people rather than waiting for them to visit JBG offices.
"For me, there is no point amending the Legal Aid and Public Defence Bill if people do not know where to seek protection and assistance.
"I have also instructed the director-general to ensure that these services are provided in all areas, including constituencies represented by the opposition. We will send letters to every member of parliament so that they can choose suitable dates according to their respective states," she said.
Earlier in her speech, Azalina said the opening of the JBG Pengerang Satellite Office marked a strategic step by the Madani government in strengthening access to justice through legal aid services that are closer, more accessible, and more responsive to the needs of the people.
She said the establishment of the satellite office, which resulted from a collaboration with the Office of the Chief Registrar of the Federal Court of Malaysia, would reduce the need for members of the public to travel long distances, thereby saving time and costs.
"The opening of the JBG Pengerang Satellite Office will bring legal aid services closer to the people, in line with the Madani government's commitment to enhancing access to justice, because justice is not a privilege but a right of every citizen, ensuring that no one is left behind by the nation's justice system," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia ready to deploy peacekeepers if UN extends Unifil mandate, says Khaled
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: Malaysia may continue deploying a peacekeeping force to Lebanon under the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil) mission, if required.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the matter is being discussed with the United Nations (UN) following Lebanon's deteriorating security situation due to the ongoing conflict with Israel.
"Malaysia had previously planned to end its peacekeeping involvement as soon as the Unifil mandate expires this December.
"The National Security Council (NSC) had earlier decided that the Unifil mandate would end in December, meaning we would bring everyone back.
"However, Israel's continuous attacks have raised questions about whether the UN peacekeeping force needs to be maintained in Lebanon to prevent the security situation in the country from worsening," he said.
"Now, the NSC is considering what to do regarding Lebanon, and if it is decided that Unifil needs to continue, Malaysia is ready to send a contingent," he told a press conference after launching the ministry's Jiwa Murni Madani programme at SK Johor Kampong here today.
Khaled said UN representatives had held discussions with Malaysia regarding the future of the Unifil mission and the need to increase the preparedness of the peacekeepers.
"We held discussions with a UN representative. One was on ensuring our personnel have better preparation than what is currently in place.
"Instead of just being peacekeepers, they are now more exposed to being in the middle of conflict and war. That is why we discussed the need for better preparation for our personnel," he said.
Commenting on the injuries sustained by two Malaysian Battalion (Malbatt) personnel recently, he said they only suffered minor injuries from shrapnel.
"The injuries suffered were not serious and treatment was given in Lebanon as our force has its own medical facilities there.
"We are under UN command. We have been briefed on the threats and challenges. So, we have advised them to take precautionary measures. We are thankful that there were no loss of lives, as in the case of the other peacekeepers," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police probe viral footage of man assaulting domestic helper in Johor Bahru
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, June 13 — Police are probing a man for violently assaulting a female domestic helper after she was caught allegedly beating his son in viral video footage.
Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said authorities are aware of the incident that was captured on video and posted on social media.
“Police have been alerted on the matter and the case is still in the early stages of investigation,” he said in a brief reply when contacted by the media today.
Earlier, a man had allegedly assaulted a female domestic helper in what was a minute and 32-second long Facebook video.
The man, who questioned the domestic helper for beating his son, was seen punching and slapping the domestic helper in a living room setting.
A woman, believed to be the man’s wife, was seen pacing around while the assault took place.
Apart from the man and his wife, there were other voices in the video that admonished the domestic helper as she covered her face from physical blows.
It is understood that the man had earlier caught the domestic helper for beating his son on a hidden close circuit television (CCTV) camera.
It is unknown who captured the video of the incident that later uploaded on Facebook.
According to information found in a Threads post, the incident was said to have taken place recently at a residence in Taman Daya here.

Title: National cohesion more important than any singular agenda, says PM Anwar
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, June 13 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today reminded Malaysians not to become caught up in narratives or actions that divide people along racial, regional and religious lines.
Speaking at the National Unity Week celebration here today, he called on Malaysians to remain vigilant against factors that could undermine national unity among the country’s diverse communities.
“Do not be careless with matters that can fracture unity,” he said, adding that nation-building requires patience, wisdom and mutual understanding as different communities progress at varying paces.
Anwar paid tribute to founding leaders such as Tunku Abdul Rahman and Tun Mustapha Harun, noting that their vision for a united Malaysia was forged with clarity of purpose despite complex social realities and without the benefit of modern theoretical frameworks.
“They had a strong sense of purpose and collective spirit that embraced all communities, including minorities,” he said.
Describing Malaysia as an “imagined community” shaped by shared aspirations, he said early leaders from places such as Kapit, Keningau and Manik Urai envisioned a united future despite limited exposure to the wider world.
However, he cautioned against prioritising narrow ethnic interests over national identity, questioning whether the country could be defended by focusing solely on individual communities.
“Sowing hatred is disrespectful and dangerous,” he said.
Drawing on Islamic teachings, Anwar said diversity in race, language and culture is part of divine design meant to foster understanding and appreciation among people.
He stressed that unity requires continuous effort and maturity, noting that it is easier to destroy than to build.
Acknowledging criticism he has faced, Anwar said inclusivity should not be seen as a weakness but as a strength essential to Malaysia’s future.

Title: Hadi says Hamzah Zainudin to resume role as Opposition Leader
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — Larut MP Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin will return as Opposition Leader just two months after relinquishing the post, PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang announced today.
Speaking at the Reset convention in Tanah Merah, Kelantan, Hadi said PAS had consulted lawmakers from within the party as well as MPs aligned with Hamzah before reaching the decision.
“We sought the views of MPs, not just PAS. Therefore, PAS has agreed to appoint Hamzah as Opposition Leader,” he was quoted as saying by FMT.
Hadi also announced that Datuk Seri Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar would remain chairman of Perikatan Nasional (PN).
According to FMT, Hadi said PAS had taken into account the views of lawmakers aligned with Hamzah, including 13 MPs who continue to sit with Bersatu.
“PAS has also decided to maintain Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar as Perikatan Nasional chairman,” Hadi said.
Ahmad Samsuri, who serves as PAS vice-president and Terengganu menteri besar, assumed the PN chairmanship in February after Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin stepped aside from the position.
Ahmad Samsuri was later named Opposition Leader following PN’s Supreme Council meeting on May 16.
Hamzah had relinquished the role after being expelled from Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia, before Ahmad Samsuri succeeded him as Opposition Leader.
Earlier at the same convention, Hamzah announced that he and the Reset movement would operate under Parti Wawasan Negara, a platform for cooperation with PAS and other like-minded parties.

Title: Two women arrested after infant’s body found outside Petaling Jaya baby hatch
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — Police have arrested two women in their 20s to assist investigations into the discovery of a baby’s body outside a baby hatch in Petaling Jaya yesterday.
Petaling Jaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsudin Mamat said officers received a report at 8.10am regarding the discovery outside the facility on Jalan Bukit Gasing.
“A post-mortem examination on the baby has been conducted, and the doctor confirmed that the baby had died following a miscarriage.
“The baby was also confirmed to have died more than 24 hours earlier,” he said in a statement today.
Shamsudin said further investigations led police to arrest the two women, both in their 20s, who are suspected of being involved in the incident.
“Neither of the suspects has any prior criminal record.
“Police have also seized several items to assist in the investigation,” he said.
He said the court had granted a two-day remand order until tomorrow for the first suspect, while the second suspect was remanded for three days until June 15 to facilitate investigations.
The case is being investigated under Section 318 of the Penal Code for concealing a birth by secretly disposing of the body of a child, an offence punishable by up to two years’ imprisonment, a fine, or both.
Members of the public with information related to the incident are urged to come forward at the nearest police station or contact the Petaling Jaya district police headquarters operations room at 03-7966 2222 to assist in the investigation.

Title: Hulu Langat MP says demolition of Sungai Tekali Rohingya settlement underway, to be completed in two weeks
Article:
HULU LANGAT, June 13 — The demolition of settlement structures housing Rohingya citizens in Kampung Baru Sungai Makau, Tekali, here is expected to take two weeks, said Hulu Langat Member of Parliament Mohd Sany Hamzan.
He said the process began at the back of the area, involving structures built on government reserve land and a portion of private land.
However, he noted that the three-story building that went viral on social media will not be demolished for the time being because it was built entirely on private land.
“I have been informed that the landowner needs two weeks to demolish the single-story structures at the back, and not the building rumored to be a three-story flat or apartment that went viral previously,” he said when contacted today.
A survey by Bernama at the location found that the landowner had begun demolishing part of the building structures at the settlement starting at 9 am today, utilising two workers and an excavator.
The survey also found that the operation did not involve the three-story building that had gone viral on social media.
Meanwhile, a Rohingya citizen met at the location informed that only one family was renting the building before the issue went viral.
Hussein, 31, who works as a house builder, said he used to live in the building between 2018 and 2020, paying a monthly rent of RM500.
“In 2020, officials from the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) and the Hulu Langat District Council ordered us to move out,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Hajiji says Sabah Southern Madani Link will strengthen energy security and drive development
Article:
SIPITANG, June 13 — The Sabah Southern Madani Link (SSML) project will serve as a catalyst for Sabah’s economic development through the provision of a more stable, robust and high-quality electricity supply system to meet the state’s future development needs, said Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor.
He said the long-awaited project is not merely an electricity transmission infrastructure project but also a strategic investment that will have a major impact on the stability and sustainability of the state’s energy sector.
He said it will strengthen the state’s electricity transmission network, enhance grid system stability and reduce the risk of power disruptions that could affect economic activities and public well-being.
“This project is also crucial in bringing Sabah into its next phase of development, where various strategic sectors such as manufacturing, the oil and gas industry, downstream processing, tourism, modern agriculture, digital data centres and technology-based industries can benefit from a stable, sufficient and high-quality energy supply.
“Therefore, the development of the energy sector is no longer merely about meeting current needs but has become the foundation of the state’s future planning,” he said when speaking at the launch of the SSML project by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim at the Lundayeh Cultural Association Complex here today.
Hajiji said the project, approved by the federal government, demonstrates the Prime Minister’s continued commitment to supporting Sabah’s development agenda, particularly in meeting the people’s basic needs through more resilient energy infrastructure.
He said the implementation of the project also reflects the close cooperation between the state and federal governments in ensuring Sabah has a more stable, robust and high-quality electricity supply system.
Hajiji said the state government has charted the direction of the energy sector through the Sabah Energy Roadmap and Master Plan 2040, which is centred on three main pillars: energy security, equitable access to energy and energy sustainability.
The plan serves as the government’s primary guide in ensuring that energy supplies are not only sufficient to meet current needs but are also capable of supporting the state’s long-term economic growth.
According to him, the development of renewable energy resources will continue to be intensified, as Sabah has tremendous potential to emerge as a green energy hub on the island of Borneo.
“With abundant hydropower resources, high levels of solar irradiation throughout the year and vast biomass potential, Sabah is strategically positioned to lead clean energy development in the region.
“This potential is not only capable of attracting high-value investments but also creating new economic opportunities and generating more jobs for the people,” he said.
According to Hajiji, strong energy infrastructure is crucial to supporting the development of strategic industrial areas such as the Sipitang Oil and Gas Industrial Park Kota Kinabalu Industrial Park, Palm Oil Industrial Cluster Lahad Datu, as well as various industrial zones and economic growth hubs being planned across the state.
He said all these areas require a stable and sufficient electricity supply to support industrial activities and attract more domestic and international investments.
“The Sabah Southern Madani Link project will not only enhance the reliability of electricity supply between regions but also provide the capacity needed to integrate more renewable energy sources into Sabah’s grid system in the future,” he said.
Hajiji also expressed appreciation to the Prime Minister for his support in implementing the project, as well as to all parties involved in making it a reality.
“May this project become the foundation for a brighter future for Sabah, with a stable energy supply, a rapidly growing economy and progress that can be enjoyed by all the people of this state,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Fahmi urges Media Council to work with social media platforms against fake news ahead of state polls
Article:
KUALA PILAH, June 13 — The Malaysian Media Council (MMC) has been urged to meet with social media platforms to coordinate measures against fake news and misleading content ahead of the state elections in Johor and Negeri Sembilan.
Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the move is vital amid growing concerns over the spread of misinformation, including the misuse of local media logos to generate fake graphics and reports on social media.
“I discussed with the MMC recently, and they expressed apprehension over the same issue: fake news and the misuse of local media logos to generate fabricated graphics.
“Insya-Allah, I am ready to cooperate with the MMC and our media friends to ensure that the spread of fake news on social media is minimised or completely eradicated,” he told reporters after launching the AI@Nadi programme at the Seri Menanti National Information Dissemination Centre here today.
He added that a joint initiative with the MMC to tackle fake news during the state polls will be announced at the upcoming National Journalists’ Day (HAWANA) celebration in Penang.
Fahmi said social media platforms must also play a more proactive role by taking immediate action against user-reported fake content, especially regarding elections.
“Certain platforms, such as TikTok, already feature a dedicated reporting button for election misinformation or false content regarding elections,” he said.
Meanwhile, he said 62 Nadi centres are operating in Negeri Sembilan, running various community programmes such as the simultaneous state-wide rollout of the WIRA AI initiative today, aimed at providing early exposure to the ethical and effective use of AI, including prompting skills, particularly for students.
The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) provides RM19 million a year to sustain nationwide Nadi operations and digital literacy initiatives, the minister noted.
He added that the government aims to produce at least one prompt engineer at every Nadi centre nationwide to strengthen local digital capabilities.
“We do not want to be mere consumers of foreign applications; instead, we want to produce local AI developers, especially among high school leavers,” he said.
He said Nadi will continue to serve as an official government information hub for initiatives such as BUDI MADANI, Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (SARA) and Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR).
He said the government is also working with the Information Department to ensure accurate information is disseminated nationwide through the Nadi network.
He added that Nadi facilities will also be opened to media practitioners as field workstations to ease reporting and news gathering at the grassroots level, including during state polls. — Bernama

Title: Fahmi: Pakatan Harapan must focus on its own strengths, not opponents’ weaknesses
Article:
KUALA PILAH, June 13 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) should not rely on the weaknesses of its opponents but instead focus on its own strengths in facing the upcoming state elections, said PH Communications Director Datuk Fahmi Fadzil.
He said the leadership’s directive was clear in that the party machinery must be strengthened based on its own capabilities without depending on external factors.
